title: Fix `q[1:$]` when queue has only one item in it
body:
```
module top;
    initial begin
        automatic int foo[$] = {1};
        $display("%0d: %p", foo.size(), foo);
        foo = foo[1:$];
        $display("%0d: %p", foo.size(), foo);
    end
endmodule
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

With verilator, this is the output of the above code:
```
1: '{'h1}
1: '{'h1}
```
The SystemVerilog spec says that code should act just like calling foo.pop_front(), in section 7.10.4:
```
q = q[1:$];  // void'(q.pop_front()) or q.delete(0)
```
A commercial simulator running the same code outputs this:
```
# 1: '{1}
# 0: '{}
```
What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
```
verilator -j "$(nproc)" --trace-threads 2 --trace-fst -o sim --binary queue_size.sv && obj_dir/sim
```
What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?
```
$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-100-g996ae35a1
```
Not the very latest git master, but close-ish.

What OS and distribution are you using?

Rocky Linux 9

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Sure
